title: Feed-backing stamps should be mutable to avoid bucket exhaustion from frequent updates
content:
## Claim

Stamps used to back **feed writes** (manifests) should be created **mutable** — immutable stamps waste one bucket slot per feed update and will exhaust long before the nominal capacity.

## Evidence

- Each feed update is a new SOC → a new chunk that consumes a bucket slot under immutable semantics
- With immutable batches, the batch fails when any single bucket fills (non-uniform distribution means this happens well before 2^depth chunks)
- Mutable batches replace the oldest chunk in an overflowing bucket — old feed indices are garbage-collected automatically, keeping capacity stable

## How

```js
bee.createPostageBatch(amount, depth, { immutableFlag: false })
```

## Why it matters

Our adapter writes frequent manifest updates (every 3-second debounce flush). Running these on immutable stamps is a time-bomb: the batch eventually fails on hot-bucket exhaustion long before its TTL expires. Mutable stamps are the correct primitive for this workload.
description: Immutable stamps waste a new bucket slot on every feed update. Creating stamps with immutableFlag: false reuses slots and prevents exhaustion.
